g.title: All analysis types available in ROSS. Each analysis is called via Rotor.run_*() and returns a specific results object.
Calculates the static deflection of the shaft under self-weight and element loads. Returns → StaticResults
Solves the complex eigenvalue problem: [M]ẍ + ([C] + Ω[G])ẋ + [K]x = 0 Returns → ModalResults Natural frequencies, damping ratios, mode shapes
Sweeps rotational speeds and calculates natural frequencies at each speed. Returns → CampbellResults Identifies critical speeds
Frequency response function (FRF). H(ω) = [-ω²M + iω(C+ΩG) + K]⁻¹ F Returns → FrequencyResponseResults
Synchronous unbalance response. Sweeps rotational speed with unbalance mass at specific nodes. Returns → ForcedResponseResults
Time integration of the complete system. Methods: state-space or Newmark. Supports time-varying forces. Returns → TimeResponseResults
Undamped Critical Speed Map. Varies bearing stiffness and calculates undamped critical speeds. Returns → UCSResults
Log-log map: critical speeds vs. bearing stiffness. Shows actual bearing stiffness as a vertical reference line.
API 617 Level 1 stability analysis. Calculates applied cross-coupled stiffness (Qa) and compares with stability threshold (Q0). If Qa < Q0, the rotor is stable. Returns → Level1Results
Q0 vs. Qa plot with pass/fail criterion
Harmonic Balance Method (HBM). Periodic steady-state response with nonlinearities (e.g., nonlinear bearings). More efficient than time integration. Returns → HarmonicBalanceResults
Rotor-stator contact. Models radial impact and tangential friction. Returns → TimeResponseResults
Transverse crack (breathing crack). Stiffness varies with rotation. Generates 2× and 3× speed harmonics. Returns → TimeResponseResults
Angular or parallel misalignment. 1× and 2× excitation forces. Returns → TimeResponseResults
Static deflection curve of the shaft
Shearing force diagram
Bending moment diagram
Free body diagram with bearing reactions
Animated 3D mode shape (precession)
2D mode shape (amplitude vs axial position)
Mode orbit at a specific node
Full Campbell diagram Frequencies vs. speed + synchronous line
Campbell with mode shape thumbnails next to each curve. Identifies FW/BW.
FRF amplitude vs. frequency
FRF phase vs. frequency
Polar diagram (Nyquist) of the FRF
Bode diagrams (amplitude and phase vs. speed)
2D deflected shape of the rotor
3D deflected shape of the rotor with orbits
Displacement vs. time for a single DOF
Orbit (x vs. y) at a node
Orbits along the shaft (3D)
FFT of the time signal (frequency spectrum)
All analysis types available in ROSS. Each analysis is called via Rotor.run_*() and returns a specific results object.
Calculates the static deflection of the shaft under self-weight and element loads. Returns → StaticResults
Solves the complex eigenvalue problem: [M]ẍ + ([C] + Ω[G])ẋ + [K]x = 0 Returns → ModalResults Natural frequencies, damping ratios, mode shapes
Sweeps rotational speeds and calculates natural frequencies at each speed. Returns → CampbellResults Identifies critical speeds
Frequency response function (FRF). H(ω) = [-ω²M + iω(C+ΩG) + K]⁻¹ F Returns → FrequencyResponseResults
Synchronous unbalance response. Sweeps rotational speed with unbalance mass at specific nodes. Returns → ForcedResponseResults
Time integration of the complete system. Methods: state-space or Newmark. Supports time-varying forces. Returns → TimeResponseResults
Undamped Critical Speed Map. Varies bearing stiffness and calculates undamped critical speeds. Returns → UCSResults
Log-log map: critical speeds vs. bearing stiffness. Shows actual bearing stiffness as a vertical reference line.
API 617 Level 1 stability analysis. Calculates applied cross-coupled stiffness (Qa) and compares with stability threshold (Q0). If Qa < Q0, the rotor is stable. Returns → Level1Results
Q0 vs. Qa plot with pass/fail criterion
Harmonic Balance Method (HBM). Periodic steady-state response with nonlinearities (e.g., nonlinear bearings). More efficient than time integration. Returns → HarmonicBalanceResults
Rotor-stator contact. Models radial impact and tangential friction. Returns → TimeResponseResults
Transverse crack (breathing crack). Stiffness varies with rotation. Generates 2× and 3× speed harmonics. Returns → TimeResponseResults
Angular or parallel misalignment. 1× and 2× excitation forces. Returns → TimeResponseResults
